body:
Except where the circumstances prescribed in paragraph (1B) apply to the claimant, where the claimant's capital calculated in accordance with this Part exceeds £6,000 it shall be treated as equivalent to a weekly income of £1 for each complete £250 in excess of £6,000 but not exceeding £16,000.
Where the circumstances prescribed in paragraph (1B) apply to the claimant and that claimant's capital calculated in accordance with this Part exceeds £10,000, it shall be treated as equivalent to a weekly income of £1 for each complete £250 in excess of £10,000 but not exceeding £16,000.
For the purposes of paragraph (1A), the prescribed circumstances are that the claimant lives permanently in a care home or an independent hospital, an Abbeyfield Home, or specified Polish Resettlement Act accommodation where the claimant requires personal care.
Notwithstanding paragraphs (1) and (1A), where any part of the excess is not a complete £250 that part shall be treated as equivalent to a weekly income of £1.
For the purposes of paragraphs (1) and (1A), capital includes any income treated as capital under regulations 48 and 60.
heading: Regulation 53
